mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 8.0 percent, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth expectations, boosting consumer spending and driving immediate positive equity returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 8.0 percent over a 5-year test period.

mechanism_narrative: Strong real GDP growth combined with rising industrial production signals synchronized demand-supply expansion, which compresses credit spreads and lifts equity risk premiums over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising GDPC1 and INDPRO fails to predict positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of out-of-sample observations.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery regime, persistent low federal funds rates combined with stable consumer price inflation anchor long-term discount rates, producing sustained positive equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns turn negative when FEDFUNDS remains below 0.5% and CPIAUCSL rises above 2.5% year-over-year for two consecutive quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts reflect immediate consumer confidence and construction sector activity, providing a direct positive catalyst for equity markets over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: Monthly Spearman correlation between HOUST month-over-month changes and 5-day SPX returns falls below 0.05 over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production accelerates by more than 0.5 percent in a single month, supply chain bottlenecks ease and corporate margins expand, triggering positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of months where INDPRO exceeds the 0.5 percent monthly growth threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and declining unemployment rates validate a self-reinforcing labor demand cycle, which sustains positive equity returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: The combined signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark over any rolling 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansion phases, rising real retail sales combined with steady GDP growth indicate peak consumer demand, which historically drives short-term equity outperformance before mean reversion.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns turn negative when RSAFS growth decelerates below 1 percent annualized while GDPC1 growth exceeds 3 percent annualized.
